Class StreamProcessor

java.lang.Object
com.semanticprivacyguard.stream.StreamProcessor
public final class StreamProcessor extends Object
Memory-efficient, line-by-line PII redaction over streams and files.

Why this matters

The standard SemanticPrivacyGuard.redact(String) API loads the entire input into a single String. For a 50 MB log file that means ~50 MB on the heap for the raw text, plus another 100–150 MB for the tokenizer's working strings — 150–200 MB per concurrent request. On a Lambda with 512 MB of RAM handling 10 concurrent calls that is an OOM event waiting to happen.

StreamProcessor processes one line at a time: each line string is detected, redacted, written to the output, and then immediately eligible for garbage collection. Heap usage stays roughly constant regardless of document size — bounded by the size of the longest single line (typically < 4 KB in log files).

Token numbering

Counters are document-scoped, not line-scoped. If line 3 contains alice@example.com it gets [EMAIL_1]; if line 7 contains bob@example.com it gets [EMAIL_2]. The same value never produces two identical tokens within the same document.

Quick start

SemanticPrivacyGuard spg = SemanticPrivacyGuard.create();

// File-to-file redaction — constant heap regardless of file size
StreamRedactionSummary summary =
    spg.redactPath(Path.of("access.log"), Path.of("access.redacted.log"));

System.out.println(summary);
// → StreamRedactionSummary[lines=84231, linesWithPII=312, matches=389, timeMs=740]

// InputStream / OutputStream
try (InputStream  in  = new FileInputStream("raw.txt");
     OutputStream out = new FileOutputStream("clean.txt")) {
    spg.redactStream(in, out);
}

// Reader / Writer (e.g. inside a servlet filter)
spg.redactStream(request.getReader(), response.getWriter());

// Lazy Java Stream — integrates with Files.lines() or any Stream<String>
try (Stream<String> lines = Files.lines(inputPath)) {
    spg.streamProcessor()
       .redactLines(lines)
       .forEach(System.out::println);
}

Thread safety

StreamProcessor instances are thread-safe. Each call to redact() or redactLines() maintains its own independent document-scoped counter state and writes to its own output sink; multiple threads may use the same instance concurrently without coordination.

Since:
1.1.0
Author:
Hemant Naik

  • Constructor Details

    • StreamProcessor

      public StreamProcessor(SPGConfig config)
      Creates a StreamProcessor from the given configuration.

      The same configuration used to create a SemanticPrivacyGuard instance may be passed here so that both APIs behave identically.

      Parameters:
      config - the detection and redaction configuration (never null)

  • Method Details

    • redact

      public StreamRedactionSummary redact(Reader reader, Writer writer) throws IOException
      Reads lines from reader, redacts PII in each line, and writes the sanitised lines to writer.

      Both streams are left open after this call; the caller is responsible for closing them (use try-with-resources). The writer is flushed but not closed.

      Line terminators are normalised: each output line is followed by the platform line separator (System.lineSeparator()). The final line does not receive a trailing newline if the source did not have one — the implementation uses a write-before pattern.

      Parameters:
      reader - the source of lines (never null)
      writer - the redaction output sink (never null)
      Returns:
      aggregated StreamRedactionSummary for the full document
      Throws:
      IOException - if any I/O error occurs

    • redact

      public StreamRedactionSummary redact(InputStream in, OutputStream out, Charset charset) throws IOException
      Byte-stream variant of redact(Reader, Writer). Reads from in and writes to out using the given charset.
      Parameters:
      in - source byte stream (never null)
      out - output byte stream (never null)
      charset - character encoding to use (never null)
      Returns:
      aggregated StreamRedactionSummary
      Throws:
      IOException - if any I/O error occurs

    • redact

      public StreamRedactionSummary redact(InputStream in, OutputStream out) throws IOException
      Byte-stream variant using UTF-8 encoding.
      Parameters:
      in - source byte stream (never null)
      out - output byte stream (never null)
      Returns:
      aggregated StreamRedactionSummary
      Throws:
      IOException - if any I/O error occurs

    • redact

      public StreamRedactionSummary redact(Path inputFile, Path outputFile) throws IOException
      File-to-file redaction. Reads from inputFile, writes the redacted output to outputFile (which is created or overwritten), and returns aggregated statistics.

      Both files are handled with try-with-resources so they are always closed, even on error.

      StreamRedactionSummary s =
    processor.redact(Path.of("server.log"), Path.of("server.clean.log"));
System.out.println(s);
      Parameters:
      inputFile - path to the source file (must exist and be readable)
      outputFile - path to the destination file (created if absent)
      Returns:
      aggregated StreamRedactionSummary
      Throws:
      IOException - if either file cannot be opened or an I/O error occurs

    • redactLines

      public Stream<String> redactLines(Stream<String> lines)
      Returns a lazy Stream<String> where each element is the redacted version of the corresponding input line.

      Token numbering is document-scoped: counters are shared across the entire stream, so each value receives a globally unique token ([EMAIL_1], [EMAIL_2], …) even when PII appears on different lines.

      Parallel streams are not supported. Document-scoped counters require sequential processing to guarantee deterministic token numbers. If a parallel stream is passed this method throws IllegalArgumentException. Call stream.sequential() before passing if needed.

      This method integrates naturally with Files.lines(Path, Charset):

      try (Stream<String> lines = Files.lines(inputPath)) {
    processor.redactLines(lines)
             .forEach(outputWriter::println);
}

      Note: unlike redact(Reader, Writer), this method does not return a StreamRedactionSummary; use the Reader/Writer or Path API when you need statistics.

      Parameters:
      lines - a sequential stream of input lines (never null)
      Returns:
      a lazy stream of redacted lines in the same order
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if lines.isParallel() is true
